_meta.sourceDocument: AQA GCSE Computer Science (8525) Specification — first teaching September 2025, first exams Summer 2027

_meta.pageRef: Section 2.1 Subject content — Specification at a glance

topics.title: 3.1 Fundamentals of algorithms
topics.description: Paper 1 topic. Covers representing algorithms, efficiency of algorithms, searching algorithms, and sorting algorithms.

subtopics.title: 3.1.1 Representing algorithms
subtopics.description: Understand and explain algorithms, decomposition, and abstraction. Use pseudo-code, program code and flowcharts. Determine purpose of simple algorithms using trace tables.

subtopics.title: 3.1.2 Efficiency of algorithms
subtopics.description: Understand that more than one algorithm can solve the same problem. Compare efficiency of algorithms (time efficiency only).

subtopics.title: 3.1.3 Searching algorithms
subtopics.description: Understand and explain linear search and binary search algorithms. Compare and contrast advantages and disadvantages of both.

subtopics.title: 3.1.4 Sorting algorithms
subtopics.description: Understand and explain merge sort and bubble sort algorithms. Compare and contrast advantages and disadvantages of both.

topics.title: 3.2 Programming
topics.description: Paper 1 topic. Covers data types, programming concepts, arithmetic/relational/Boolean operations, data structures, I/O, string handling, random numbers, structured programming, and robust/secure programming.

subtopics.title: 3.2.1 Data types
subtopics.description: Understand and use integer, real, Boolean, character, and string data types appropriately.

subtopics.title: 3.2.2 Programming concepts
subtopics.description: Use variable/constant declaration, assignment, iteration, selection, and subroutines. Definite and indefinite iteration, nested selection and iteration. Use meaningful identifier names.

subtopics.title: 3.2.3 Arithmetic operations in a programming language
subtopics.description: Use addition, subtraction, multiplication, real division, integer division (DIV) and remainder (MOD).

_meta.pageRef: Section 3.2.3 Arithmetic operations in a programming language

subtopics.title: 3.2.4 Relational operations in a programming language
subtopics.description: Use equal to, not equal to, less than, greater than, less than or equal to, and greater than or equal to operators.

_meta.pageRef: Section 3.2.4 Relational operations in a programming language

subtopics.title: 3.2.5 Boolean operations in a programming language
subtopics.description: Use NOT, AND, and OR Boolean operators within conditions for iterative and selection structures.

_meta.pageRef: Section 3.2.5 Boolean operations in a programming language

subtopics.title: 3.2.6 Data structures
subtopics.description: Understand data structures. Use one and two-dimensional arrays. Use records in the design of solutions.

subtopics.title: 3.2.7 Input/output
subtopics.description: Obtain user input from keyboard. Output data and information to the computer display.

subtopics.title: 3.2.8 String handling operations in a programming language
subtopics.description: Use length, position, substring, concatenation, character/character code conversion, and string conversion operations (string↔integer, string↔real).

_meta.pageRef: Section 3.2.8 String handling operations in a programming language

subtopics.title: 3.2.9 Random number generation in a programming language
subtopics.description: Be able to use random number generation within computer programs.

_meta.pageRef: Section 3.2.9 Random number generation in a programming language

subtopics.title: 3.2.10 Structured programming and subroutines
subtopics.description: Understand subroutines (procedures/functions), parameters, return values, local variables, and the structured approach to programming. Explain advantages of structured programming.

subtopics.title: 3.2.11 Robust and secure programming
subtopics.description: Write simple data validation and authentication routines. Understand testing, types of test data (normal, boundary, erroneous), and types of error (syntax, logic). Identify and categorise errors in algorithms and programs.

topics.title: 3.3 Fundamentals of data representation
topics.description: Paper 2 topic. Covers number bases, converting between number bases, units of information, binary arithmetic, character encoding, representing images, representing sound, and data compression.

subtopics.title: 3.3.1 Number bases
subtopics.description: Understand decimal (base 10), binary (base 2) and hexadecimal (base 16). Computers use binary to represent all data and instructions. Explain why hexadecimal is often used in computer science.

subtopics.title: 3.3.2 Converting between number bases
subtopics.description: Convert between binary, decimal and hexadecimal (values 0–255). Convert in both directions between binary↔decimal, binary↔hexadecimal, and decimal↔hexadecimal.

subtopics.title: 3.3.3 Units of information
subtopics.description: Know that a bit is the fundamental unit of information, a byte is 8 bits. Know decimal prefixes: kilo (kB = 1,000 bytes), mega (MB), giga (GB), tera (TB).

subtopics.title: 3.3.4 Binary arithmetic
subtopics.description: Add up to three binary numbers (max 8-bit). Apply logical binary shifts and describe situations where shifts can be used (multiplication/division by powers of 2).

subtopics.title: 3.3.5 Character encoding
subtopics.description: Understand character sets and describe 7-bit ASCII and Unicode encoding. Describe purpose and advantages of Unicode over ASCII. Use encoding tables to convert characters to/from character codes.

subtopics.title: 3.3.6 Representing images
subtopics.description: Understand pixels and bitmaps. Describe image size, colour depth. Calculate bitmap file sizes (Size = W × H × D bits). Convert binary data to/from bitmap images.

subtopics.title: 3.3.7 Representing sound
subtopics.description: Understand analogue-to-digital conversion of sound. Describe sampling rate and sample resolution. Calculate sound file sizes (bits = rate × resolution × seconds).

subtopics.title: 3.3.8 Data compression
subtopics.description: Explain data compression and why it is used. Explain Huffman coding and interpret Huffman trees. Calculate bits saved by Huffman compression. Explain and represent run-length encoding (RLE) using frequency/data pairs.

topics.title: 3.4 Computer systems
topics.description: Paper 2 topic. Covers hardware/software, Boolean logic, software classification, programming languages and translators, and systems architecture.

subtopics.title: 3.4.1 Hardware and software
subtopics.description: Define hardware and software and understand the relationship between them.

subtopics.title: 3.4.2 Boolean logic
subtopics.description: Construct truth tables for NOT, AND, OR, XOR gates. Construct/interpret truth tables for simple logic circuits (up to 3 inputs). Create, modify and interpret logic circuit diagrams. Create and interpret Boolean expressions using NOT, AND, OR and XOR.

subtopics.title: 3.4.3 Software classification
subtopics.description: Explain system software and application software. Understand the need for and functions of operating systems (processor, memory, I/O, applications, security management) and utility programs.

subtopics.title: 3.4.4 Classification of programming languages and translators
subtopics.description: Know low-level and high-level languages. Explain machine code and assembly language. Understand interpreters, compilers, and assemblers. Advantages/disadvantages of low-level vs high-level programming.

_meta.pageRef: Section 3.4.4 Classification of programming languages and translators

subtopics.title: 3.4.5 Systems architecture
subtopics.description: Explain role of main memory and CPU components: ALU, control unit, clock, registers, buses. Effect of clock speed, cores, and cache on CPU performance. Fetch-Execute cycle. Types of memory: RAM, ROM, Cache, Registers. Secondary storage (solid state, magnetic). Cloud storage. Embedded systems.

topics.title: 3.5 Fundamentals of computer networks
topics.description: Paper 2 topic. Covers network types (PAN, LAN, WAN), wired and wireless networks, network protocols (TCP, IP, HTTP, HTTPS, SMTP, IMAP), network security, and the TCP/IP 4-layer model.

subtopics.title: 3.5.1 Networks and topologies
subtopics.description: Define computer networks. Describe PAN, LAN and WAN. Advantages/disadvantages of networks. Wired vs wireless networks (copper, fibre). Network protocols: TCP, IP, HTTP, HTTPS, SMTP, IMAP. Network security: authentication, encryption, firewall, MAC address filtering. TCP/IP 4-layer model: application, transport, internet and link layers.

topics.title: 3.6 Cyber security
topics.description: Paper 2 topic. Covers fundamentals of cyber security, cyber security threats, and methods to detect and prevent threats.

subtopics.title: 3.6.1 Fundamentals of cyber security
subtopics.description: Define cyber security and describe its main purposes. Cyber security consists of processes, practices and technologies protecting networks, computers, programs and data from attack, damage or unauthorised access.

subtopics.title: 3.6.2 Cyber security threats
subtopics.description: Explain social engineering (blagging/pretexting, phishing, shouldering), malicious code/malware (viruses, trojans, spyware), pharming, weak/default passwords, misconfigured access rights, removable media, unpatched software. Explain penetration testing (insider and external attack simulation).

subtopics.title: 3.6.3 Methods to detect and prevent cyber security threats
subtopics.description: Explain biometric measures, password systems, CAPTCHA, email confirmations, and automatic software updates as security measures.

_meta.pageRef: Section 3.6.3 Methods to detect and prevent cyber security threats

topics.title: 3.7 Relational databases and SQL
topics.description: Paper 2 topic. Covers relational database concepts and structured query language (SQL).

_meta.pageRef: Section 3.7 Relational databases and structured query language (SQL)

subtopics.title: 3.7.1 Relational databases
subtopics.description: Explain concepts of database and relational database. Understand table, record, field, data type, primary key, and foreign key. Understand that relational databases eliminate data inconsistency and redundancy.

subtopics.title: 3.7.2 Structured query language (SQL)
subtopics.description: Use SQL to retrieve data: SELECT, FROM, WHERE, ORDER BY ASC|DESC (up to two tables). Insert data: INSERT INTO ... VALUES. Edit and delete data: UPDATE ... SET ... WHERE; DELETE FROM ... WHERE.

topics.title: 3.8 Ethical, legal and environmental impacts of digital technology
topics.description: Paper 2 topic. Covers ethical, legal and environmental impacts and risks of digital technology on wider society, including issues of privacy. Exam topics include cyber security, mobile technologies, wireless networking, cloud storage, hacking, wearable technologies, computer-based implants, and autonomous vehicles.

_meta.pageRef: Section 3.8 Ethical, legal and environmental impacts of digital technology on wider society, including issues of privacy

subtopics.title: 3.8.1 Ethical, legal and environmental impacts
subtopics.description: Explain current ethical, legal and environmental impacts and risks of digital technology on society, including privacy issues. Areas: cyber security, mobile technologies, wireless networking, cloud storage, hacking, wearable technologies, computer-based implants, autonomous vehicles.

_meta.pageRef: Section 3.8 Ethical, legal and environmental impacts of digital technology on wider society

papers.title: Paper 1: Computational thinking and programming skills

sections.title: Computational thinking and programming skills
sections.description: Content drawn from sections 3.1 (Fundamentals of algorithms) and 3.2 (Programming). Mix of multiple choice, short answer and longer answer questions assessing programming, practical problem-solving and computational thinking skills.

sections.title: Computing concepts
sections.description: Content drawn from sections 3.3–3.8. Mix of multiple choice, short answer, longer answer and extended response questions assessing SQL programming skills and theoretical knowledge.

objectives.title: AO1
objectives.description: Demonstrate knowledge and understanding of the key concepts and principles of computer science.

_meta.pageRef: Section 4.2.1 Assessment objective weightings for GCSE Computer Science

objectives.title: AO2
objectives.description: Apply knowledge and understanding of key concepts and principles of computer science.

_meta.pageRef: Section 4.2.1 Assessment objective weightings for GCSE Computer Science

objectives.title: AO3
objectives.description: Analyse problems in computational terms: to make reasoned judgements and to design, program, evaluate and refine solutions.

_meta.pageRef: Section 4.2.1 Assessment objective weightings for GCSE Computer Science
